Usage: # Default execution (based on current month) tkctl analyze recommend # Analysis based on a specific month tkctl analyze recommend --month 2026-01 Output example: 📊 Data Analysis Recommendation Report ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Target Month: 2026-01 ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 📈 [1] Media Control Logs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Table: TPU_ISTAT_MEDIA_EVENT_LOG Current Month: 55,941 | Previous Month: 28,970 | Growth Rate: +93.1% ⚠️ 93% increase over previous month - analysis recommended → Recommended command: tkctl analyze media-top --month 2026-01 ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 📈 [2] Event Logs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Table: TPU_ISTAT_EVENT_LOG Current Month: 503 | Previous Month: 566 | Growth Rate: -11.1% ✅ Within normal range ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 🔍 Summary: 1 item requires analysis, 2 items normal Assessment criteria: 🔴 Surge (Priority 1): 100% or more increase over previous month - immediate analysis required ⚠️ Rapid Increase (Priority 2): 50-100% increase over previous month - analysis recommended ✅ Normal (Priority 3): Less than 50% change from previous month

Analyzes the Top N occurrence frequency by process+path combination in media control event logs. Helps identify which processes accessed which paths most frequently. Usage: # Top 10 for the current month tkctl analyze media-top --limit 10 # Analysis for a specific month tkctl analyze media-top --month 2026-01 # Save results to CSV file tkctl analyze media-top --month 2026-01 --output media_report.csv Output example: 2026-01-15 17:51:59 [INFO] Media control log analysis (media-top) 2026-01-15 17:51:59 [INFO] Period: 2026-01-01 ~ 2026-02-01 2026-01-15 17:51:59 [INFO] Source: opensearch 2026-01-15 17:51:59 [INFO] Limit: 5 entries ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- RANK PROCESS PATH COUNT ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1 C:\Windows\Explorer.EXE 42,349 2 C:\Windows\system32\svchost.exe 5,966 3 C:\Program Files\...\MsMpEng.exe E:\ 2,611 4 1,019 5 C:\Windows\System32\powershell.exe \RaiDrive\MyDS\ 811 ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Total 5 entries displayed

Detects agents that are logging excessively compared to the average. Helps identify cases where abnormal volumes of events are generated from specific PCs. Usage: # Default execution (detect agents logging 5x or more above average) tkctl analyze agent-anomaly # Adjust threshold (detect only agents logging 10x or more above average) tkctl analyze agent-anomaly --threshold 10 # Analysis for a specific month tkctl analyze agent-anomaly --month 2026-01 Output example: 2026-01-15 17:52:01 [INFO] Agent anomaly logging detection (agent-anomaly) 2026-01-15 17:52:01 [INFO] Period: 2026-01-01 ~ 2026-02-01 2026-01-15 17:52:01 [INFO] Source: opensearch 2026-01-15 17:52:01 [INFO] Threshold: average x5.0 or above -------------------------------------------- 📊 Agent Logging Statistics -------------------------------------------- Total agents: 150 Average log count: 352.1 Standard deviation: 1,245.3 Anomalous agents: 3 (threshold: average x5.0 or above) -------------------------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------------------------- RANK AGENT_ID(PUID) COUNT RATIO ---------------------------------------------------------------------- 1 a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-... 15,234 43.3x 🔴 2 b2c3d4e5-f6a7-8901-... 8,456 24.0x 🔴 3 c3d4e5f6-a7b8-9012-... 2,108 6.0x ⚠️ ---------------------------------------------------------------------- Assessment criteria: 🔴 Critical: 10x or more above average logging ⚠️ Warning: 5-10x above average logging

Aggregates log occurrence counts by process and displays the top N. Helps identify which processes are generating the most events. Usage: # Top 20 processes tkctl analyze process-top # Display only top 5 tkctl analyze process-top --limit 5 # Analysis for a specific month and save to file tkctl analyze process-top --month 2026-01 --output process.csv Output example: 2026-01-15 17:52:03 [INFO] Per-process log aggregation (process-top) 2026-01-15 17:52:03 [INFO] Period: 2026-01-01 ~ 2026-02-01 2026-01-15 17:52:03 [INFO] Source: opensearch 2026-01-15 17:52:03 [INFO] Limit: 5 entries ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- RANK PROCESS COUNT RATIO ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1 C:\Windows\Explorer.EXE 42,349 80.3% 2 C:\Windows\system32\svchost.exe 5,966 11.3% 3 C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\Windows Defender\...\MsMpEng.exe 2,611 4.9% 4 1,019 1.9% 5 C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe 811 1.5% ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Total 5 entries displayed (aggregate total: 52,756 entries)

Analyzes the growth trends of DB and log data to support storage capacity planning. Two modes are available: Fast Mode (default) and Precise Mode. Usage: # Default execution (Fast Mode: instant analysis based on information_schema) tkctl analyze db-trend # Precise analysis (Precise Mode: detailed analysis based on COUNT(*) queries, takes time) tkctl analyze db-trend --precise tkctl analyze db-trend -P # Save results to CSV file tkctl analyze db-trend --output trend.csv 1. Fast Mode (Default)​ Uses information_schema metadata to return results instantly (O(1)). Does not impose load on the running DB and provides an integrated capacity report for the entire system (MariaDB + OpenSearch + Disk). Output example: 2026-01-19 22:36:07 [INFO] DB data growth trend analysis (db-trend) 2026-01-19 22:36:07 [INFO] ⚡ Fast analysis mode (information_schema based) ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 📊 System Capacity Status Report (Fast Mode) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 📁 MariaDB Size : 138.29 MB (disk usage 0.1%) 📁 OpenSearch : 57.80 MB 📁 Install Dir : 71.07 GB (/usr/local/TACHYON/TTS40) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 💾 Disk Available : 27.86 GB (71.8% used of total 98.93 GB) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 📈 Monthly Est. : 16.30 MB / month (MariaDB: 10.52 MB + OpenSearch: 5.78 MB) 📈 Yearly Est. : 195.59 MB / year 📅 Disk Saturation : 🟢 Estimated saturation in ~1750.6 months (2171-11-19) 📋 Tables Analyzed : 50 ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 📋 Table Status (top 10 of 50 total) ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- # TABLE ROWS (est.) SIZE AVG_ROW ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1 TPU_ISTAT_MEDIA_EVENT_LOG 76,989 91.19 MB 1.21 KB 2 TPU_ISTAT_EVENT_LOG 1,079 1.02 MB 986 B 3 TPU_ISTAT_AGENT_INTEGRITY_LOG 528 592.00 KB 1.12 KB ... 10 TPU_ISTAT_SUSB_INTEGRITY_LOG 16 256.00 KB 16.00 KB ... 40 more tables 2,182 9.81 MB - ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Key features: Integrated capacity report: View MariaDB, OpenSearch, install directory, and disk availability at a glance Capacity Planning: Estimate monthly/yearly growth and predict disk saturation based on current data size Top 50 large tables: Detailed display of top 10 tables by size with summary of the rest 2. Precise Mode (Detailed Analysis)​ Activated with the --precise or -P option. Performs actual COUNT(*) queries to analyze exact row counts and growth trends by period (1 week, 1 month, 1 quarter, 1 year). May take time for large databases. Output example: 2026-01-17 20:21:17 [INFO] 🔍 Precise analysis mode (using COUNT queries, may take time) ... 📊 DB Capacity Planning Report ... ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- TABLE PERIOD START(ACC) END(ACC) INCREASE RATE TREND ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- STAT_MEDIA_EVENT_LOG Last 1 week 71,499 96,792 25,293 +35.4% 🟠 Rapid increase (caution) STAT_MEDIA_EVENT_LOG Last 1 month 16,268 96,792 80,524 +495.0% 🔴 Surge (danger) ... Key features: Accurate data: Based on actual row counts rather than InnoDB estimates Period-based trends: Calculates exact growth rates (%) by comparing with data from 1 week/1 month/1 quarter/1 year ago Risk assessment: Trend diagnosis based on growth rate (🔴 Surge, 🟠 Rapid increase, etc.)

tkctl provides a variety of commands for installing, operating, and maintaining the TACHYON system. This section summarizes the functionality and key flags of all commands. For detailed usage, please refer to each item in the side menu.

1. Basic System Management (tkctl ...) Details: 3.1 Basic System Management Command Function Key Flags (Options) version View detailed system and component versions None info View system resources and disk usage None admin Reset administrator password [user_id] (default: admin) completion Generate shell auto-completion scripts [bash/zsh/fish/powershell] update Service update and package management [service] -f <file> (update) package [service] (create package) 2. Service Management (tkctl service ...) Details: 3.2 Service Management Command Function Key Flags (Options) check Check status of all services None start Start a service [service] (all if omitted) stop Stop a service [service] (all if omitted) restart Restart a service [service] (all if omitted) jvm JVM memory optimization --set [auto/S/M/L/size] (apply setting) --dry-run (preview) loglevel View/set log level [service] --set [LEVEL] logrotate Log rotation management --set (auto-apply recommended settings) 3. Monitoring and Analysis (tkctl size, tkctl analyze ...) Details: 3.3 Monitoring and Analysis Command Subcommand Function Key Flags (Options) size tables MariaDB table capacity [count], --by-month (monthly aggregation) size opensearch OpenSearch index capacity --by-month (monthly aggregation) analyze recommend Analysis recommendation report --month (target month) analyze media-top Media control frequency analysis --month, --limit, --output (CSV export) analyze agent-anomaly Agent anomaly logging detection --month, --threshold (threshold) analyze process-top Per-process log aggregation --month, --limit analyze db-trend DB data growth trend --output 4. Backup and Restore (tkctl backup, tkctl restore ...) Details: 3.4 Backup and Restore Command Subcommand Function Key Flags (Options) backup mariadb MariaDB backup full, incr, log, config (type selection) backup mariadb partition Partition management list, drop, backup backup opensearch Create snapshot --init-repo (initialize repository — ⚠️ auto-restarts OpenSearch if path.repo missing) backup opensearch index Index management list, delete, backup backup opensearch ilm ILM policy management status, apply restore mariadb MariaDB restore --target-date, --target-dir restore mariadb partition Partition restore --table, --partition, --file restore opensearch Snapshot restore [snapshot], --indices 5. Environment Configuration (tkctl env ...) Details: 3.5 Environment Configuration Command Function Key Flags (Options) (default) Full environment configuration status summary --set (apply all recommended values) selinux SELinux settings [mode], --set ulimit System limit (Open Files) settings [value], --set firewall Firewall port opening [port], --set ssh SSH port change [port], --set web Web (HTTPS) port change [port], --set db DB (MariaDB) port change [port], --set (batch apply to related services) 6. Security Analysis and Remediation (tkctl analyze security, tkctl fix security) Details: Security Analysis and Remediation Command Function Key Flags (Options) analyze security Security vulnerability analysis --target (target), --format (HTML report), --quiet fix security Vulnerability remediation --id (vulnerability ID), --dry-run (simulation), --backup
tkctl provides integrated security vulnerability analysis (analyze) across OS, Web (Nginx, etc.), and DB (MariaDB) areas, as well as automated remediation (fix) for discovered vulnerabilities.
Inspects the current system configuration state according to KISA Critical Information Infrastructure Vulnerability Analysis criteria and generates a report.

Flag Description Default --target, -t Specify inspection target (os, web, db, all) all --format, -f Specify report format (json, html) json --output, -o File path to save results Auto-generated --quiet, -q Suppress console output (for automation scripts) false

1. Full system analysis with HTML report generation tkctl analyze security --format html # Result: tkctl-security-report-20260118-143000.html generated Open the generated HTML file in a browser to view intuitive inspection results distinguished by color codes. 2. Quick OS configuration check only tkctl analyze security -t os 3. CI/CD pipeline integration (JSON output) tkctl analyze security --format json --quiet # Result: tkctl-security-report-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.json generated (no logs)

Automatically modifies settings for items found to be 'Vulnerable' in the analysis results to strengthen security. Caution: Security remediation directly modifies system settings. It is strongly recommended to perform a backup (--backup=true) and preview changes with --dry-run mode first.

Flag Description Default --id Specific vulnerability ID to remediate (e.g., U-01) Required (except when using --report or --all) --report Automated remediation based on analysis report (JSON) "" --all Batch remediation of all supported items (use with caution) false --dry-run Simulate remediation without actual changes false --backup Backup original configuration files before remediation true --interactive, -i Interactive mode requesting confirmation for each item false

1. Simulate remediation for a specific vulnerability (U-01) with dry-run tkctl fix security --id U-01 --dry-run # Result: # [INFO] Security remediation process started # [INFO] [U-01] Remediation started: Setting PermitRootLogin to 'no' in SSH config file (sshd_config) and restarting service. # [INFO] [DryRun] No actual changes will be made. # [SUCC] All remediation processes completed. 2. Actual remediation for a specific vulnerability (U-01) tkctl fix security --id U-01 # Result: # [INFO] [U-01] Remediation started: Setting PermitRootLogin to 'no' in SSH config file (sshd_config) and restarting service. # [SUCC] Backup complete: /etc/ssh/sshd_config.bak_20260118_194500 # [SUCC] Remediation complete 3. Batch remediation based on report (--report) Selects and remediates only vulnerable (VULNERABLE) items based on analysis results (tkctl analyze --format json). tkctl fix security --report tkctl-security-report-20260118.json # Result: # [INFO] Analyzing report: tkctl-security-report-20260118.json # Vulnerabilities found: 15 | Auto-remediable: 3 # ... (remediation process continues) 4. Remediation in interactive mode (--interactive) Requests user confirmation before each item. Options: y (Yes), n (No/Skip), a (All/Yes for all remaining), q (Quit/Abort) tkctl fix security --all --interactive # Result: # [INFO] 85 remediation items selected. # ---------------------------------------------------------------- # [INFO] [U-01] Remediation target: Restrict root remote access (SSH PermitRootLogin setting) # [U-01] Proceed with remediation? [y(Yes)/n(No)/a(All)/q(Quit)]: y # [INFO] -> Starting remediation... # [SUCC] Backup complete: /etc/ssh/sshd_config.bak_20260119_143000 # [SUCC] Remediation complete # ...

tkctl performs diagnostics following the KISA Critical Information Infrastructure Technical Vulnerability Analysis and Assessment Detailed Guide for the items listed below. 3.6.3.1 Linux OS​ All 73 items supported for remediation (v0.4.17) Automated Remediation (Type A): U-01U-04, U-07U-12, U-15, U-18U-23, U-28U-30, U-32, U-37, U-39 Manual Guide (Type C): Remaining items - detailed commands and Q. I want to revert after remediation caused an issue. A. The fix command always creates a backup (*.bak) of the original file during execution. Overwrite the original with the backup file or manually restore the contents. Example: cp /etc/ssh/sshd_config.bak_20260118 /etc/ssh/sshd_config

Automated Analysis Notice (v0.4.24+) Starting from v0.4.24, automated analysis and remediation for all 85 KISA 2026 Critical Information Infrastructure technical vulnerability items has been implemented. Therefore, most items in this manual can be automatically processed with the tkctl analyze security command, and one-click remediation is available via tkctl fix security. For detailed implementation specifications, refer to the KISA 2026 Security Vulnerability Implementation Specification. This document is maintained as a reference for legacy environments or special situations where automated analysis is not possible. When tkctl analyze security results occasionally show a 'Manual inspection required (MANUAL)' status or when cross-verification is needed, refer to this guide. Note: This document is based on the KISA Critical Information Infrastructure Technical Vulnerability Analysis and Assessment Detailed Guide.

U-01. Root Remote Access Restriction​ Item Details Category Account Management Risk Level High Inspection Target Remote access services such as SSH, Telnet Inspection Method # Check SSH configuration grep -i "PermitRootLogin" /etc/ssh/sshd_config # Check Telnet configuration (if in use) cat /etc/securetty Compliant Criteria PermitRootLogin no is set Remediation Method # Change SSH configuration sudo sed -i 's/^#*PermitRootLogin.*/PermitRootLogin no/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config sudo systemctl restart sshd U-02. Password Complexity Settings​ Item Details Category Account Management Risk Level High Inspection Target PAM configuration Inspection Method # Check PAM complexity configuration cat /etc/pam.d/system-auth | grep pam_pwquality cat /etc/security/pwquality.conf Compliant Criteria Combination of alphabetic, numeric, and special characters Minimum 8 characters Remediation Method # /etc/security/pwquality.conf settings minlen = 8 dcredit = -1 ucredit = -1 lcredit = -1 ocredit = -1 U-03. Account Lockout Threshold Settings​ Item Details Category Account Management Risk Level High Inspection Target PAM faillock configuration Inspection Method grep -E "deny=|faillock" /etc/pam.d/system-auth grep -E "deny=|faillock" /etc/pam.d/password-auth Compliant Criteria Account lockout after 5 or fewer login failures Remediation Method # faillock configuration example auth required pam_faillock.so preauth deny=5 unlock_time=600 auth [default=die] pam_faillock.so authfail deny=5 unlock_time=600 U-04. Password File Protection​ Item Details Category Account Management Risk Level High Inspection Target /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow Inspection Method # Check whether Shadow file is in use awk -F: '$2 != "x" && $2 != "!" && $2 != "*" {print $1}' /etc/passwd Compliant Criteria All account passwords are encrypted and stored in /etc/shadow U-05. No UID 0 Except Root​ Item Details Category Account Management Risk Level High Inspection Target /etc/passwd Inspection Method awk -F: '$3 == 0 {print $1}' /etc/passwd Compliant Criteria No accounts with UID 0 other than root U-06. Root Home/Path Permissions​ Item Details Category File Permissions Risk Level High Inspection Target su command permissions Inspection Method ls -l /usr/bin/su cat /etc/pam.d/su | grep pam_wheel Compliant Criteria su command is restricted to the wheel group U-07 ~ U-09. Password Policy Inspection​ Item Inspection Target Recommended Setting U-07 Minimum Length 8+ characters (PASS_MIN_LEN 8) U-08 Maximum Age 90 days or less (PASS_MAX_DAYS 90) U-09 Minimum Age 1 day or more (PASS_MIN_DAYS 1) Inspection Method cat /etc/login.defs | grep -E "PASS_MIN_LEN|PASS_MAX_DAYS|PASS_MIN_DAYS" U-10 ~ U-14. Account Management Inspection​ ID Inspection Item Inspection Command U-10 Unnecessary Accounts cat /etc/passwd | grep -E "lp:|uucp:|nuucp:" U-11 Admin Group grep "^root" /etc/group U-12 Orphaned Groups find / -nogroup -print 2>/dev/null U-13 Duplicate UIDs awk -F: '{print $3}' /etc/passwd | sort | uniq -d U-14 User Shells grep -E "^nobody|^bin|^daemon" /etc/passwd U-15. Session Timeout Settings​ Inspection Method echo $TMOUT cat /etc/profile | grep TMOUT Compliant Criteria TMOUT set to 600 or less Remediation Method echo "export TMOUT=600" >> /etc/profile source /etc/profile U-16 ~ U-27. File/Directory Permission Inspection​ ID Inspection Target Recommended Permission Inspection Command U-16 PATH Environment Variable No '.' included echo $PATH U-17 Files Without Owners None find / -nouser -print 2>/dev/null U-18 /etc/passwd 644 (root) ls -l /etc/passwd U-19 /etc/shadow 400 (root) ls -l /etc/shadow U-20 /etc/hosts 600 (root) ls -l /etc/hosts U-21 /etc/xinetd.conf 600 (root) ls -l /etc/xinetd.conf U-22 /etc/rsyslog.conf 644 (root) ls -l /etc/rsyslog.conf U-23 /etc/services 644 (root) ls -l /etc/services U-24 SUID/SGID Remove from unnecessary files find / -perm -4000 -print 2>/dev/null U-25 User Environment Files Appropriate permissions ls -la ~/.bashrc ~/.profile U-26 World Writable None find / -perm -2 -type f 2>/dev/null U-27 /dev Devices Only valid files find /dev -type f 2>/dev/null U-28 ~ U-35. Service Management Inspection (1)​ ID Inspection Item Inspection Command U-28 .rhosts File find / -name ".rhosts" 2>/dev/null U-29 TCP Wrapper cat /etc/hosts.allow /etc/hosts.deny U-30 hosts.lpd ls -l /etc/hosts.lpd U-31 NIS Service systemctl status ypserv ypbind U-32 UMASK Setting grep UMASK /etc/login.defs U-33 Home Directory Permissions ls -ld /home/* U-34 Home Directory Existence awk -F: '{print $6}' /etc/passwd | xargs ls -d 2>&1 U-35 Hidden Files find / -name ".*" -type f 2>/dev/null U-36 ~ U-51. Service Management Inspection (2)​ ID Inspection Item Compliant Criteria Inspection Command U-36 Finger Service Disabled systemctl status finger U-37 Anonymous FTP Disabled grep anonymous_enable /etc/vsftpd/vsftpd.conf U-38 r-series Services Disabled systemctl status rsh rlogin rexec U-39 Cron File Permissions 600 (root) ls -l /etc/cron.allow /etc/cron.deny U-40 DoS Vulnerable Services Disabled grep -E "echo|discard|daytime" /etc/xinetd.d/* U-41 NFS Service Only when needed systemctl status nfs U-42 NFS Access Control Properly configured cat /etc/exports U-43 automountd Disabled systemctl status autofs U-44 RPC Service Disabled rpcinfo -p localhost U-45 NIS, NIS+ Disabled systemctl status ypserv U-46 tftp, talk Disabled systemctl status tftp talk U-47 Sendmail Version Latest version sendmail -d0.1 -bv root 2>&1 | head -1 U-48 Spam Relay Restricted postconf | grep smtpd_relay_restrictions U-49 Sendmail Execution Restricted ls -l /usr/sbin/sendmail U-50 DNS Version Latest version named -v U-51 Zone Transfer Restricted grep allow-transfer /etc/named.conf U-52 ~ U-70. Service Management Inspection (3)​ ID Inspection Item Compliant Criteria Inspection Command U-52 Apache Directory Listing Indexes removed grep -r "Indexes" /etc/httpd/ U-53 Apache Process Privileges Non-root execution ps -ef | grep httpd U-54 Apache Parent Directory AllowOverride None grep AllowOverride /etc/httpd/conf/httpd.conf U-55 Apache Unnecessary Files Manuals removed ls /var/www/html/manual/ U-56 Apache Symlink Restriction FollowSymLinks removed grep FollowSymLinks /etc/httpd/conf/httpd.conf U-57 Apache File Upload LimitRequestBody set grep LimitRequestBody /etc/httpd/conf/httpd.conf U-58 Apache Document Root Separation Separate directory grep DocumentRoot /etc/httpd/conf/httpd.conf U-59 SSH Remote Access Enabled systemctl status sshd U-60 FTP Service Only when needed systemctl status vsftpd U-61 FTP Account Shell Restricted grep -E "ftp:|ftpuser:" /etc/passwd U-62 Ftpusers File Permissions 640 (root) ls -l /etc/vsftpd/ftpusers U-63 Ftpusers Configuration root included cat /etc/vsftpd/ftpusers U-64 at File Permissions 640 (root) ls -l /etc/at.allow /etc/at.deny U-65 SNMP Service Only when needed systemctl status snmpd U-66 SNMP Community No 'public' grep community /etc/snmp/snmpd.conf U-67 Login Banner Configured cat /etc/issue /etc/issue.net U-68 NFS Config File Permissions 644 (root) ls -l /etc/exports U-69 expn, vrfy Restriction Disabled postconf | grep disable_vrfy_command U-70 Apache Information Hiding ServerTokens Prod grep ServerTokens /etc/httpd/conf/httpd.conf U-71 ~ U-73. Patch and Log Management​ ID Inspection Item Inspection Method U-71 Latest Security Patches yum check-update --security or apt list --upgradable U-72 Regular Log Review Verify log review policies and history (documents/processes) U-73 System Logging Configuration cat /etc/rsyslog.conf - verify policy

Nginx web server security configuration inspection items. Nginx Configuration File Locations​ # Common paths /etc/nginx/nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/*.conf # Tachyon TTS environment /usr/local/TACHYON/TTS40/nginx/conf/nginx.conf N-01. Version Information Disclosure Restriction​ Item Details Category Information Disclosure Risk Level Medium Inspection Method grep -i "server_tokens" /etc/nginx/nginx.conf curl -I http://localhost 2>&1 | grep Server Compliant Criteria server_tokens off; is set Remediation Method # Inside the http block of nginx.conf http { server_tokens off; } N-02. HTTP Method Restriction​ Item Details Category Access Control Risk Level Medium Inspection Method grep -i "limit_except" /etc/nginx/nginx.conf curl -X OPTIONS http://localhost -I Compliant Criteria Unnecessary methods (PUT, DELETE, TRACE, etc.) are blocked Remediation Method location / { limit_except GET POST { deny all; } } N-03. Directory Listing Restriction​ Item Details Category Access Control Risk Level High Inspection Method grep -i "autoindex" /etc/nginx/nginx.conf Compliant Criteria autoindex off; or no setting (default is off) Remediation Method # Remove autoindex on; or change to off autoindex off; N-04. File Upload Restriction​ Item Details Category Configuration Management Risk Level Medium Inspection Method grep -i "client_max_body_size" /etc/nginx/nginx.conf Compliant Criteria Limited to an appropriate size (e.g., 10M) Remediation Method http { client_max_body_size 10M; } N-05. Hidden File Access Restriction​ Item Details Category Access Control Risk Level Medium Inspection Method grep -E "location.*\\\\." /etc/nginx/nginx.conf curl http://localhost/.htaccess Compliant Criteria Access to files starting with . is blocked Remediation Method location ~ /\. { deny all; } N-06. Log Configuration Check​ Item Details Category Log Management Risk Level Medium Inspection Method grep -E "access_log|error_log" /etc/nginx/nginx.conf ls -l /var/log/nginx/ Compliant Criteria Both access_log and error_log are enabled Remediation Method http { access_log /var/log/nginx/access.log; error_log /var/log/nginx/error.log warn; }

MariaDB/MySQL database security configuration inspection items. MariaDB Configuration File Locations​ # Common paths /etc/my.cnf /etc/mysql/my.cnf /etc/my.cnf.d/*.cnf # Tachyon TTS environment /usr/local/TACHYON/TTS40/mariadb/my.cnf D-01. Default Account Management​ Item Details Category Account Management Risk Level High Inspection Method -- Check for anonymous accounts SELECT User, Host FROM mysql.user WHERE User=''; -- Check for test databases SHOW DATABASES LIKE 'test%'; Compliant Criteria No anonymous accounts or test databases exist Remediation Method -- Delete anonymous accounts DROP USER ''@'localhost'; DROP USER ''@'%'; -- Delete test database DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS test; -- Or run mysql_secure_installation D-02. Root Remote Access Restriction​ Item Details Category Account Management Risk Level High Inspection Method SELECT User, Host FROM mysql.user WHERE User='root'; Compliant Criteria root account Host allows only 'localhost' or '127.0.0.1' Remediation Method -- Remove remote root access DELETE FROM mysql.user WHERE User='root' AND Host NOT IN ('localhost', '127.0.0.1', '::1'); FLUSH PRIVILEGES; D-03. Configuration File Permissions​ Item Details Category Configuration Management Risk Level Medium Inspection Method ls -l /etc/my.cnf ls -l /var/lib/mysql/ Compliant Criteria Configuration file: 640 or less (owned by root/mysql) Data directory: 750 (owned by mysql) Remediation Method chmod 640 /etc/my.cnf chown root:mysql /etc/my.cnf chmod 750 /var/lib/mysql chown mysql:mysql /var/lib/mysql D-04. Password Complexity​ Item Details Category Password Risk Level Medium Inspection Method SHOW VARIABLES LIKE 'validate_password%'; -- Or SHOW VARIABLES LIKE 'simple_password_check%'; Compliant Criteria Password validation plugin is enabled Remediation Method -- MariaDB INSTALL PLUGIN simple_password_check SONAME 'simple_password_check'; -- MySQL INSTALL COMPONENT 'file://component_validate_password'; SET GLOBAL validate_password.policy = MEDIUM; D-05. Network Binding Configuration​ Item Details Category Network Risk Level High Inspection Method grep bind-address /etc/my.cnf /etc/my.cnf.d/* netstat -tlnp | grep 3306 Compliant Criteria bind-address = 127.0.0.1 or restricted to a specific IP Remediation Method # my.cnf [mysqld] section [mysqld] bind-address = 127.0.0.1 D-06. Audit Log Configuration​ Item Details Category Log Management Risk Level Medium Inspection Method grep -E "log_error|server_audit" /etc/my.cnf ls -l /var/log/mysql/ Compliant Criteria Error log is enabled Audit plugin usage is recommended Remediation Method # my.cnf [mysqld] section [mysqld] log_error = /var/log/mysql/error.log # Audit plugin (optional) plugin-load = server_audit=server_audit.so server_audit_logging = ON server_audit_file_path = /var/log/mysql/audit.log

Command Function Key Flags (Options) check Check all service statuses None start Start a service [service] : Service name (all if omitted) stop Stop a service [service] : Service name (all if omitted) restart Restart a service [service] : Service name (all if omitted) jvm JVM memory optimization --set : Setting value (auto, S, M, L, 4G, etc.) --dry-run : Simulation --no-restart : Skip restart loglevel View/set log level [service] : Target service --set : Set level (INFO, DEBUG, etc.) logrotate Log rotation management --set : Auto-apply recommended settings Checks and controls the status of TACHYON and middleware services. Each subcommand (check, start, stop, restart, loglevel, jvm, logrotate) supports independent help (-h).

check: Check status of all services start <service>: Start a service stop <service>: Stop a service restart <service>: Restart a service jvm: JVM heap memory configuration management and auto-optimization loglevel: View/set log level logrotate: Log rotation management

Middleware Services: mariadb - MariaDB database redis - Redis cache nginx - NGINX web server zookeeper - Zookeeper kafka - Kafka message broker opensearch - OpenSearch search engine opensearch-dashboards - OpenSearch Dashboards logstash-kafka-os - Logstash TACHYON Services: TACHYON-Api1 - API server TACHYON-Auth1 - Authentication server TACHYON-Manager1 - Management server TACHYON-Stat1 - Statistics server TACHYON-Report1 - Report server TACHYON-Batch1 - Batch server TACHYON-Watchdog1 - Watchdog server Special Options: all - All services (sequential start/stop)

# Check all service statuses tkctl service check # Start a specific service tkctl service start mariadb tkctl service start TACHYON-Api1 # Start all services (sequential execution workflow) tkctl service start all /* Execution Order Details */ 1. Level 1: Base Infrastructure (Infra) - zookeeper - redis - mariadb - opensearch - opensearch-dashboards (3-second wait) 2. Level 2: Messaging (Kafka) - kafka (5-second wait & connection status verification) 3. Level 3: Log Collection and Web Server - logstash-kafka-os - nginx 4. Level 4: TACHYON API - TACHYON-Api* (all discovered instances: Api1, Api2...) (60-second wait - API initialization) 5. Level 5: TACHYON Service Logic - TACHYON-Auth* - TACHYON-Manager* - TACHYON-Stat* - TACHYON-Report* - TACHYON-Batch* (10-second wait) 6. Level 6: Watchdog - TACHYON-Watchdog* # Stop a specific service tkctl service stop nginx # Stop all services (reverse workflow) tkctl service stop all (Safely shuts down in order: Watchdog -> Service Logic -> API -> Logs/Web -> Kafka -> Infra)

Checks or changes the logging level (DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, etc.) of each running service. Analyzes configuration files of Java-based TACHYON services and major middleware (Nginx, Redis, etc.) to display the currently applied level, and allows immediate changes through the --set option. Usage: # Check logging level of all services tkctl service loglevel # Check logging level of a specific service tkctl service loglevel api # Change logging level of a specific service tkctl service loglevel api --set DEBUG tkctl service loglevel auth --set INFO tkctl service loglevel batch --set WARN Supported Levels: DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, TRACE, OFF Diagnosis Target Files and Details: TACHYON Services: *.yml_dev (plaintext originals), logback-spring.xml, log4j2.xml Displays system logging level based on logging.level.* settings. NGINX: nginx/conf/nginx.conf Error Log: Extracts the level (DEBUG, INFO, ERROR, etc.) set in the error_log directive. Access Log: Since NGINX access logs have no level control and only manage recording status, parses the access_log off; setting to display as ON/OFF. Redis: redis.conf (loglevel directive) Extracts Redis standard levels (DEBUG, VERBOSE, NOTICE, WARNING). Log Level Change Logic (Java Services - v0.4.22 Enhancement): Step Action Description 1 Modify *.yml_dev file Batch change all keys under logging.level.* (root, com.tachyon, etc.) 2 Copy *.yml file Reflect in the actual configuration file used by Spring Boot 3 Sync *_ORIGIN file Also update the default configuration file (settings preserved after reinstall) 4 Restart service systemctl restart TACHYON-[Svc]1.service Note: The logging.level.root key is automatically created for services that don't have it (batch, watchdog, etc.).
documents.s: 3.2.1 Service Log Level Check and Configuration (loglevel)

documents.t: Checks the log rotation configuration status of TACHYON and middleware services and automatically configures missing settings. Usage: # Check all service log rotation status tkctl service logrotate # Check log rotation status of a specific service tkctl service logrotate nginx # Auto-configure log rotation for services with missing settings tkctl service logrotate --set # Configure log rotation for a specific service tkctl service logrotate redis --set Supported Services: Middleware: nginx, redis, opensearch (main), opensearch-gc (GC logs), kafka, logstash, mariadb TACHYON: api, auth, manager, stat, batch, report, watchdog (Java/Log4j2 based) Management Tools: tkadmin, tkctl Rotation Types: logrotate: Uses the system logrotate daemon (/etc/logrotate.d/) log4j2: Uses Java Log4j2 internal rolling policy internal: Uses service's built-in rotation mechanism
documents.s: 3.2.2 Service Log Rotation Status Check and Configuration (logrotate)

documents.t: Views, changes, or auto-optimizes JVM heap memory (-Xms, -Xmx) settings for TACHYON and related middleware based on system specifications. Usage: # Check all JVM service memory settings and system allocation tkctl service jvm # Change memory settings for a specific service tkctl service jvm api --set 2G # Apply official recommended presets (XS, S, M, L) tkctl service jvm --set S # Auto-optimize based on system specs and agent count (recommended) tkctl service jvm --set auto # Auto-optimize with explicit agent count tkctl service jvm --set auto --agents 5000 # Preview changes without applying (Dry-run) tkctl service jvm --set auto --dry-run # Skip automatic service restart after configuration change tkctl service jvm --set auto --no-restart Auto-Optimization Algorithm (auto): Analyzes the server's physical memory (RAM) size and the number of managed agents (Scale) to calculate optimal ratios per service. Reflects the importance and actual load distribution of each component: OpenSearch (20%), Kafka (10%), API (5%), etc. Ensures system stability with per-service minimum heap (256MB~1GB) guarantees and maximum cap (16GB) policies. Preset Types: XS (Extra Small): For low-spec test environments with 8GB or less (ultra-lightweight settings) S (Small): For 16GB recommended specifications M (Medium): For 32GB recommended specifications L (Large): For high-spec environments with 64GB or more Safety Features: Automatically creates a backup (.bak) of the original file before any configuration change. Resource Warning: When total JVM allocation exceeds 80% of physical memory, outputs a warning message and recommends manual review for operational stability. Pre-reports to tkadmin during service restart to prevent Watchdog false alarms.
documents.s: 3.2.3 Service JVM Memory Optimization and Management (jvm)

documents.t: tkctl automatically detects the system's language settings to provide multilingual support.
Detects the system's LANG or LC_ALL environment variables. # English environment LANG=en_US.UTF-8 tkctl info # → English output # Korean environment LANG=ko_KR.UTF-8 tkctl info # → Korean output
If Korean characters appear broken due to SSH client settings or terminal font issues, you can force-switch to English by choosing one of the following methods.
documents.s: 6.2 Manual Language Setting and Forced Switching

documents.t: You can specify the language using the -l (lowercase L) or --lang flag with any command. Once you run a command with this flag, the setting is automatically saved to tkctl.ini and persists in subsequent runs without the flag. You can also enter auto as the value to delete the saved setting and restore automatic system detection mode. # Force switch to English and save setting tkctl --lang en version # Restore to automatic detection mode (delete setting) tkctl --lang auto version # Force switch to Korean and save setting tkctl -l ko version
documents.s: Method 1: Using a Flag at Runtime (One-time with Automatic Environment Persistence)

documents.t: You can directly modify the tkctl.ini file in the path where tkctl is installed to fix the language. basedir=/usr/local/TACHYON/TTS40 log_level=DEBUG language=en
documents.s: Method 2: Permanent Modification in Configuration File (tkctl.ini)

documents.t: When tkctl determines that the terminal environment cannot properly support Korean output, it automatically switches to English (en) even if the system locale is Korean (ko), preventing garbled output. Detection logic: If the LANG environment variable is Korean-based but does not contain the UTF-8 string (e.g., ko_KR.EUC-KR or ko_KR), the English language pack is automatically activated for safety. Recovery method: If the terminal supports Korean but English is displayed, run tkctl --lang ko to manually force the setting.
English (en): Default language Korean (ko): Fully translated
tkctl ensures key consistency across language-specific JSON files through automated testing.

During development, the go test ./internal/i18n/... command verifies the following: Key consistency (TestI18nKeysConsistency): Confirms that all message keys defined in ko.json and en.json match exactly 1:1. Help required keys (TestHelpKeysExist): Confirms that Long description and Example keys for all required commands exist without omission.

The following naming patterns are recommended for dynamic language switching and subcommand support, and the system automatically recognizes and applies them: Command description: [command]_short, [command]_long Example: [command]_example Subcommand: [parent]_[child]_short, [parent]_[child]_long Flag: [command]_[flag_name]_flag
You can support new languages by creating a lang directory in the tkctl execution path and adding JSON files.

Create directory mkdir lang Create language pack file vim lang/ja.json Write JSON { "meta": { "version": "1.0", "compatibility": "0.1.0" }, "messages": { "root_short": "TACHYON CLI - System Management Tool", "info_title": "### TACHYON System Information ###", "service_check_title": "### TACHYON Service Status Check ###" } } Run LANG=ja_JP.UTF-8 ./tkctl info

Language Filename Environment Variable Example English en.json en_US.UTF-8 Korean ko.json ko_KR.UTF-8 Japanese ja.json ja_JP.UTF-8 Chinese (Simplified) zh-cn.json zh_CN.UTF-8 Chinese (Traditional) zh-tw.json zh_TW.UTF-8 French fr.json fr_FR.UTF-8 German de.json de_DE.UTF-8 Spanish es.json es_ES.UTF-8 Russian ru.json ru_RU.UTF-8
If a key is missing for the requested language, it automatically falls back to English. If the language pack file does not exist, English is used.

You can manage OS system-wide locale settings using the tkctl env locale command. This is useful for fundamentally resolving Korean character corruption issues in the terminal. # Check system locale tkctl env locale # Set to Korean locale (ko_KR.UTF-8) tkctl env locale ko --set For detailed usage, refer to 5. Environment Configuration > 3.5.8 Locale Settings.
tkctl is distributed as a single binary. Download the binary suitable for your system environment and place it in an appropriate path on the server (e.g., /usr/local/bin).
After downloading the binary, you must grant execution permission. # Grant execution permission (e.g., when located in /usr/local/bin) chmod +x /usr/local/bin/tkctl

Once permissions are granted, run the following command to automatically apply tkctl integration settings to your current shell (Bash, Zsh, etc.). tkctl -i This command performs the following tasks: Auto-completion registration: Enables auto-completion via the Tab key when typing commands Shell profile update: Adds necessary environment information to ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc

If you run tkctl for the first time without performing separate initial setup, it will prompt for automatic installation. You can also explicitly remove and reinstall the configuration as follows: [root@localhost ~]# tkctl --uninstall 🗑️ Removing tkctl configuration... 📝 Removing Bash auto-completion script... ✓ /etc/bash_completion.d/tkctl removed ✓ Auto-completion check flag removed ✓ Auto-completion script successfully removed 2026-01-17 19:31:26[SUCC] Legacy configuration has been removed from /root/.bashrc. [root@localhost ~]# [root@localhost ~]# tkctl --install -y 2026-01-17 19:31:33[SUCC] Setup complete! Applied to /root/.bashrc. Run 'source /root/.bashrc' or restart your shell. [root@localhost ~]#

tkctl manages key settings through the tkctl.ini file created in the same location as the executable. Setting Key Description Default Value basedir TACHYON installation root path /usr/local/TACHYON/TTS40 admin_api_url tkadmin integration server address http://127.0.0.1:13700 log_level Log recording level (DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR) DEBUG

The URL used when tkctl sends task reports to the admin server (tkadmin) is automatically configured. Integration URL Priority: Priority 1: The admin_api_url value in tkctl.ini. Priority 2 (Auto-detection): If tkadmin.yml exists in the tkctl execution folder, reads the port setting from that file and auto-configures as http://127.0.0.1:{port}. Priority 3: Uses the default value http://127.0.0.1:13700. Communication Endpoint: Reports are sent by automatically appending the /tkadmin/api/monitor/report path to the configured address. Example: If admin_api_url is http://192.168.0.100:13700, the actual communication target is http://192.168.0.100:13700/tkadmin/api/monitor/report. When Changes Take Effect: Changes take effect immediately after modification; no separate service restart is required (as a CLI tool, it loads the configuration on each execution).
tkctl records its own logs to files and provides functionality to check and manage log rotation status for TACHYON operational services.

You can set the log level in the tkctl.ini file. Configuration file location: tkctl.ini in the tkctl execution path Configuration example: basedir=/usr/local/TACHYON/TTS40 log_level=INFO verbose=false Log levels: DEBUG: Records all logs (for development/debugging) INFO: General information and status changes (default) WARN: Warning messages only ERROR: Errors only

# View today's log tail -f /usr/local/TACHYON/TTS40/logs/tkctl_$(date +%Y%m%d).log # Last 100 lines tail -100 /usr/local/TACHYON/TTS40/logs/tkctl_$(date +%Y%m%d).log # Log for a specific date cat /usr/local/TACHYON/TTS40/logs/tkctl_20251207.log
tkctl self-logs are retained for 7 days and then automatically deleted. This policy is managed by the internal LogMaxAge constant.

In TACHYON operational environments, various middleware and services generate logs. The tkctl logrotate command allows you to view the log rotation status of all services at a glance and automatically configure them when needed.
# Check log rotation status for all services tkctl logrotate # Check a specific service only tkctl logrotate nginx # Automatically apply missing configurations tkctl logrotate --set

Service Type Service List Rotation Method Middleware nginx, redis System logrotate Middleware opensearch, kafka, logstash, mariadb Internal management TACHYON Api, Auth, Manager, Stat, Batch, Report, Watchdog Log4j2 Management Tools tkadmin tkadmin.yml configuration Management Tools tkctl Self-rotation
For detailed usage and output examples, refer to the Service Management - 3.2.2 Service Log Rotation section.

Service Log Path tkctl $BASEDIR/logs/tkctl_YYYYMMDD.log tkadmin $BASEDIR/tkadmin/logs/ NGINX $BASEDIR/nginx/logs/ Redis $BASEDIR/redis/logs/ OpenSearch $BASEDIR/opensearch/opensearch/logs/ Kafka $BASEDIR/kafka/logs/ Java Services $BASEDIR/logs/[ServiceName]/ Note: $BASEDIR defaults to /usr/local/TACHYON/TTS40.
These are global options available across all commands.

Use this when working with a path different from the default installation path. The configured value is permanently saved in the tkctl.ini file. # Change and save path tkctl -b /custom/path info # or tkctl --basedir=/custom/path info
Enables verbose output for debugging. tkctl -d service check tkctl --debug info

Removes auto-completion scripts and configuration files installed by tkctl. tkctl --uninstall # or tkctl -u Removal targets: Bash completion script (/etc/bash_completion.d/tkctl, etc.) Completion check flag file (~/.tkctl_completion_checked) Legacy .bashrc configuration blocks
Displays brief version information. tkctl -v # or tkctl --version # Output: tkctl version 0.3.19
Explicitly enters interactive shell mode for infrastructure management and testing. tkctl -s # or tkctl --shell
Running tkctl with the --shell (or -s) flag enters a go-prompt based intelligent interactive shell mode.
$ tkctl --shell # or $ tkctl -s tkctl> Note: Interactive shell mode requires a TTY (terminal) environment. Execution is restricted in pipe or redirection contexts to prevent panics.

Supports auto-completion not only for commands and subcommands but also for flags (options) and dynamic arguments. Command completion: Press TAB while typing to auto-complete and view the guide below. Flag completion: When typing - or --, displays all available options for the current command. Dynamic suggestions: When running commands like service start, retrieves and suggests the actual service list from the current system in real-time.

Polished UI: Applied a Cyan-colored prompt and a highly readable suggestion list theme. Backspace protection: Strictly protects the input area to prevent accidental deletion of the prompt region (tkctl> ). Optimized popup: View up to 15 suggestions at a glance.

Smart Ctrl+C: During command execution, only the current task is interrupted; during input waiting, only the input buffer is cleared for immediate re-input. History: Navigate previous command history using the up/down arrow keys.
You can exit the shell using the following methods: Type exit Type quit Ctrl+D (EOF)
$ tkctl -s tkctl> version tkctl version: 0.4.1 ... tkctl> service start --<TAB> --help --force --no-wait ... tkctl> exit Bye!

# Check PATH echo $PATH # Check tkctl location which tkctl # Add to PATH (temporary) export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/bin # Add to PATH (permanent) echo 'export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/bin' >> ~/.bashrc source ~/.bashrc
# Grant execution permission chmod +x tkctl # Change ownership sudo chown $USER:$USER tkctl
# Check MariaDB status tkctl service check | grep mariadb # Start MariaDB tkctl service start mariadb # Test connection mysql -u root -p

# When "latest version not found" error occurs without specifying MariaDB version # 1. Check internet connectivity curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://downloads.mariadb.org curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://archive.mariadb.org # 2. If both sites are blocked → specify version directly tkctl update package mariadb --version 10.11.16 # 3. Update to v0.5.7 or later for automatic archive.mariadb.org fallback support tkctl version
documents.s: Version Detection Failure During Package Creation

documents.t: # Reinstall completion tkctl completion bash | sudo tee /etc/bash_completion.d/tkctl # Check .bashrc grep tkctl ~/.bashrc

If TACHYON services fail to start properly or file write permission errors occur, check the SELinux configuration. # Check SELinux status tkctl env selinux # Switch SELinux to Permissive mode (logging only, blocking disabled) tkctl env selinux --set permissive
tkctl [command] [subcommand] [arguments] [flags] Tip: To enter interactive shell mode, use the --shell (or -s) flag. For details, see 4. Interactive Shell Mode.
# Full help tkctl --help # Help for a specific command tkctl service --help tkctl size --help
# Brief version information tkctl --version # Detailed version information (including middleware versions) tkctl version

Check and optimize system environment settings such as SELinux and system limits (ulimit). # Check SELinux status tkctl env selinux # Diagnose system and service limit status tkctl env ulimit # Automatically apply recommended settings tkctl env ulimit --set
